title: verify(): FMT1 guard rejects qualified TPM_RC_SIGNATURE
description: wolftpm/src/key.rs:201. Current guard: (rc & 0x80) != 0 && (rc & 0x100) == 0. FMT1 error codes encode the subject number in bits 11:8 (TPM2 Part 2 §6.6.3). Subject >= 1 sets bit 8 (0x100), so the guard incorrectly classifies a qualified TPM_RC_SIGNATURE (e.g. 0x1DB = 0x9B|0x40|0x100 for P-flag+subject=1, or 0x3DB for subject=3) as a non-FMT1 code. The mask is then NOT applied and the code falls through to the Err branch instead of returning Ok(false). Fix: change guard to (rc & 0x80) != 0 && (rc & 0xF000) == 0 (bit 7 set = FMT1 format marker; bits 15:12 clear = not a negative/extension code). VER1 codes have bit 7 clear, so they correctly fail the first half.
